questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Underground mining is one of the most hazardous occupations in the world. The primary risks include roof falls, gas explosions, flooding, and ventilation failures. In India, the Mines Act of 1952 and the Coal Mines Regulations of 2017 govern safety standards. Modern technology has significantly improved mine safety through real-time monitoring systems, gas detectors, and automated equipment. Despite these advances, safety remains a critical concern, and regular training programs are mandatory for all mine workers. The introduction of continuous miners and longwall mining methods has increased both productivity and safety.

Continuous miners and longwall mining methods have increased:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To pull someone's leg':

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Gondwana coal fields of eastern India contain the country's best quality coal. These fields stretch across Jharkhand, West Bengal, Odisha, and Chhattisgarh. The coal found here is primarily bituminous, which has high calorific value and low ash content. In contrast, the Tertiary coal fields of northeastern India contain lignite and sub-bituminous coal. Coal grading is based on ash content, with higher grades having lower ash percentages. Washeries are used to reduce ash content and improve coal quality before it is supplied to power plants and industries.

Gondwana coal fields are located in which part of India?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To play devil's advocate':
options: To argue for the opposite side
questions.solution: To argue for the opposite side

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: India's energy demand is projected to grow at 3-4% annually over the next decade. While coal remains the dominant source, accounting for about 55% of installed capacity, renewable energy is expanding rapidly. Solar power capacity has grown from just 2.6 GW in 2014 to over 70 GW in 2024. Wind power contributes another 45 GW. The government's Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for solar manufacturing aims to reduce dependence on imports. Nuclear power, with 7.5 GW capacity, and hydropower, with 47 GW, are other important components of India's energy portfolio.

Coal accounts for about what percentage of India's installed capacity?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To burn one's bridges':

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, was powered largely by coal. Steam engines, fuelled by coal, drove factories, railways, and ships, transforming economies and societies. The demand for coal led to the expansion of mining operations across Europe and America. However, the social cost was high, with poor working conditions, child labour, and environmental pollution. The legacy of the Industrial Revolution continues to influence modern industrial practices and environmental policies. Today, many former mining regions are being revitalised through diversification and clean energy initiatives.

Former mining regions are being revitalised through:

questions.stem: Meaning of 'To let the cat out of the bag':

questions.stem:
Passage: The Gondwana coal fields of eastern India contain the country's best quality coal. These fields stretch across Jharkhand, West Bengal, Odisha, and Chhattisgarh. The coal found here is primarily bituminous, which has high calorific value and low ash content. In contrast, the Tertiary coal fields of northeastern India contain lignite and sub-bituminous coal. Coal grading is based on ash content, with higher grades having lower ash percentages. Washeries are used to reduce ash content and improve coal quality before it is supplied to power plants and industries.

Tertiary coal fields of northeastern India contain:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To face the music':

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Coal is the most abundant fossil fuel in India and plays a crucial role in the country's energy mix. India has the fourth-largest coal reserves in the world, with an estimated 344 billion tonnes. Coal India Limited (CIL) is the largest coal-producing company globally, accounting for about 82% of India's coal output. However, concerns about environmental degradation and climate change have prompted a shift towards cleaner energy sources like solar and wind power. The government has set a target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.

Which of the following is mentioned as a concern related to coal usage?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To turn a blind eye':

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, was powered largely by coal. Steam engines, fuelled by coal, drove factories, railways, and ships, transforming economies and societies. The demand for coal led to the expansion of mining operations across Europe and America. However, the social cost was high, with poor working conditions, child labour, and environmental pollution. The legacy of the Industrial Revolution continues to influence modern industrial practices and environmental policies. Today, many former mining regions are being revitalised through diversification and clean energy initiatives.

Which of the following was a social cost of the Industrial Revolution?
options: Poor working conditions and child labour
Poor working conditions and child labour
questions.solution: Poor working conditions and child labour

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Underground mining is one of the most hazardous occupations in the world. The primary risks include roof falls, gas explosions, flooding, and ventilation failures. In India, the Mines Act of 1952 and the Coal Mines Regulations of 2017 govern safety standards. Modern technology has significantly improved mine safety through real-time monitoring systems, gas detectors, and automated equipment. Despite these advances, safety remains a critical concern, and regular training programs are mandatory for all mine workers. The introduction of continuous miners and longwall mining methods has increased both productivity and safety.

Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a risk in underground mining?

questions.stem: Meaning of 'The ball is in your court':
options: It is your turn to act
questions.solution: It is your turn to act

options: She was angry to her friend for lying.
She was angry on her friend for lying.
She was angry with her friend for lying.
She was angry at her friend for lying.
questions.solution: She was angry with her friend for lying.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Adding a pole to the open-loop transfer function generally:
options: Reduces stability and makes the response more oscillatory
questions.solution: An extra pole adds phase lag, pushing the root locus toward the right half-plane and reducing stability margins.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The RMS value of a sinusoidal voltage of peak value Vm is:
questions.solution: For a sine wave, RMS = Vm/√2 ≈ 0.707 Vm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The current-versus-frequency curve of a series RLC circuit peaks at the marked frequency f₀ ≈ 159 Hz. At this resonant frequency the power factor is:
questions.solution: At series resonance XL = XC, the impedance is purely resistive and current peaks, so the power factor is unity. (Here f₀ ≈ 159 Hz.)

figure.caption: Series-resonance current curve

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 3-phase synchronous motor is:
questions.solution: It produces no average starting torque and needs auxiliary (e.g. damper-winding) starting.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A sinusoidal voltage has a peak value of 200 V. Its RMS value is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The efficiency of a transformer is maximum when:
questions.solution: Maximum efficiency occurs at the load where variable copper loss = constant iron loss.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kirchhoff's Current Law (KCL) is a statement of the conservation of:
questions.solution: KCL says the algebraic sum of currents at a node is zero — charge is neither created nor destroyed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy stored in a 470 µF capacitor charged to 20 V is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An auto-transformer is more economical than a two-winding transformer mainly because:
options: Part of the power is transferred conductively, saving copper
questions.solution: In an auto-transformer only part of the power is transformed inductively; the conductive path reduces copper and size, best for ratios near 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The sinusoidal voltage shown has a peak of 311 V (marked). Its RMS value is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The commutator in a DC machine acts as a:
questions.solution: It mechanically reverses armature connections so the external current/EMF stays unidirectional.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Why can a transformer not step up a DC voltage?
options: DC cannot flow through copper windings.
Steady DC produces no changing flux, so no EMF is induced in the secondary.
DC has too high a frequency.
The core saturates instantly for all DC.
questions.solution: Transformer action needs dΦ/dt; constant DC gives zero rate of change and hence zero secondary EMF.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The capacitive reactance of a 200 µF capacitor at 60 Hz is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The synchronous speed of an AC machine with P poles at frequency f is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A series circuit has L = 0.5 H and C = 40 µF. Its resonant frequency is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy stored in an inductor carrying current I is:
questions.solution: Energy = ½LI², stored in the magnetic field of the coil.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A wattmeter reads zero on a purely reactive (inductive or capacitive) load because:
options: The average of v·i over a cycle is zero
questions.solution: Pure reactance gives a 90° phase shift; instantaneous power averages to zero over a cycle, so real power is zero.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An induction motor has air-gap power 20 kW and slip 0.04. The rotor copper loss is:
questions.solution: Rotor copper loss = s × Pag = 0.04 × 20 kW = 800 W.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The average value of a sinusoidal current over one complete cycle is:
questions.solution: Over a full cycle the positive and negative halves cancel, giving zero average.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coil has resistance 8 Ω and reactance 4 Ω. Its impedance magnitude is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a purely capacitive AC circuit, the current:
options: Is in phase with the voltage
questions.solution: An ideal capacitor makes current lead voltage by 90°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An over-excited synchronous motor draws a current that is:
questions.solution: Over-excitation makes it draw leading current — used for power-factor correction (synchronous condenser).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two resistors of 5 Ω and 12 Ω are connected in series. The equivalent resistance is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The time constant of a series RC circuit is:
questions.solution: τ = RC; after one time constant the capacitor charges to ~63% of the final voltage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 6-pole, 50 Hz induction motor runs at 970 rpm. Its slip is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The equivalent resistance of two equal resistors R in parallel is:
questions.solution: For two equal parallel resistors, Req = R·R/(R+R) = R/2.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If one wattmeter in the two-wattmeter method reads zero, the load power factor is:
questions.solution: W₂ = VLIL cos(30°+φ) = 0 when 30°+φ = 90°, i.e. φ = 60°, pf = cos60° = 0.5.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the divider shown (36 V applied), the output voltage Vout across R2 is:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 220 150" width="220" height="150"><line x1="30" y1="20" x2="30" y2="50" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="22" y="50" width="16" height="34" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="50" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">R1=2Ω</text><line x1="30" y1="84" x2="30" y2="100" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="22" y="100" width="16" height="34" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="50" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R2=8Ω</text><line x1="30" y1="134" x2="30" y2="145" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="30" cy="92" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="40" y="96" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Vout</text><text x="35" y="16" font-size="10" fill="#475569">36V</text></svg>
figure.caption: Resistive voltage divider

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Comparing series and parallel RLC resonance, which statement is correct?
options: Series resonance gives minimum impedance; parallel (anti-)resonance gives maximum impedance.
questions.solution: Series resonance → minimum Z (max current); ideal parallel resonance → maximum Z (min line current).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 24 V source drives a 10 Ω resistor. The current is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The EMF equation of a transformer is E =
questions.solution: E = 4.44 f N Φm, from the rate of change of sinusoidal flux.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Routh–Hurwitz criterion is used to determine:
options: The number of closed-loop poles in the right half-plane
questions.solution: Sign changes in the first column of the Routh array count the RHP poles (unstable roots).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A series circuit has R = 6 Ω, XL = 20 Ω, XC = 8 Ω across 200 V. The current is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If the supply frequency to a fixed RL series circuit is doubled, which statement is INCORRECT?
options: The power factor becomes more lagging.
questions.solution: Resistance is independent of frequency; only XL = 2πfL changes, so 'R doubles' is false.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the two-wattmeter method the readings are W₁ = 1000 W and W₂ = 200 W. The total 3-phase power is:
questions.solution: Total power = W₁ + W₂ = 1000 + 200 = 1200 W (the difference relates to reactive power).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Norton's theorem replaces a linear network by:
options: A current source in parallel with a resistance
A voltage source in series with a resistance
questions.solution: Norton: a current source IN in parallel with RN (= Rth).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The slip of an induction motor running at synchronous speed would be:
questions.solution: Slip s = (Ns − N)/Ns; at N = Ns, s = 0 (and torque would be zero).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A transformer delivers 50 kW with iron loss 0.5 kW and copper loss 0.6 kW. Its efficiency is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In an induction motor, the rotor copper loss equals:
questions.solution: Of the air-gap power Pag, a fraction s is dissipated as rotor copper loss and (1 − s) becomes mechanical power.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the two-wattmeter method the readings are W₁ = 1200 W and W₂ = 300 W. The total 3-phase power is:
questions.solution: Total power = W₁ + W₂ = 1200 + 300 = 1500 W (the difference relates to reactive power).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The V-curve of a synchronous motor is a plot of:
options: Armature current versus field current at constant load
questions.solution: Varying field excitation at fixed load traces a V-shaped armature-current curve, minimum at unity pf.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Electrical power is transmitted at high voltage mainly to:
questions.solution: For a given power, higher voltage means lower current, so I²R losses fall.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The impedance triangle shown has R = 3 Ω and X = 3 Ω. The impedance phase angle is about:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 200 140" width="200" height="140"><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="180" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="20" y2="10" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="140" y2="120" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="90" y="134" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R=3Ω</text><line x1="140" y1="120" x2="140" y2="50" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="145" y="85" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">X=3Ω</text><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="140" y2="50" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="70" y="78" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">Z</text></svg>

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Harmonics in a power system are primarily produced by:
options: Non-linear loads such as rectifiers and arc furnaces
questions.solution: Non-linear loads draw non-sinusoidal current, injecting harmonic frequencies into the supply.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A DC motor on 230 V draws 20 A with armature resistance 1 Ω. The back EMF is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For maximum power transfer to a resistive load from a DC source, the load resistance should equal:
questions.solution: Maximum power is transferred when RL = Rth.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The time constant of a series RL circuit is:
questions.solution: τ = L/R; the current reaches ~63% of its final value in one time constant.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The balanced Wheatstone bridge shown has P = 100 Ω, Q = 200 Ω and S = 40 Ω (galvanometer reads zero). The unknown resistance R is:
questions.solution: At balance P/Q = R/S, so R = P·S/Q = 100×40/200 = 20 Ω.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 200 160" width="200" height="160"><polygon points="100,20 180,80 100,140 20,80" fill="none" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="55" y="45" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">P=100Ω</text><text x="140" y="45" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">Q=200Ω</text><text x="55" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R=?</text><text x="135" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">S=40Ω</text><circle cx="100" cy="80" r="9" fill="none" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="100" y="84" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">G</text></svg>
figure.caption: Wheatstone bridge

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A step-up transformer has:
options: More secondary turns than primary turns
questions.solution: Step-up means N₂ > N₁, so V₂ > V₁.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A copper wire (ρ = 1.7×10⁻⁸ Ω·m) is 200 m long with 4 mm² cross-section. Its resistance is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For maximum power transfer to a complex load ZL from a source of impedance Zth, ZL must equal:
questions.solution: With a reactive source, maximum power needs ZL = Zth* so the reactances cancel and resistances match.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a balanced delta connection, the line current is related to the phase current by:
questions.solution: In delta, line and phase voltages are equal and IL = √3·Iph.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A source has Vth = 12 V and Rth = 8 Ω. The maximum power deliverable to a matched load is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a balanced star connection, the line voltage is related to the phase voltage by:
questions.solution: In star, line voltage leads phase voltage and VL = √3·Vph; line and phase currents are equal.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The inductive reactance of a 0.05 H inductor at 50 Hz is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The holding current of a thyristor is:
options: The current needed to latch it on
The minimum anode current to keep it conducting after the gate is removed
questions.solution: Holding current keeps an already-on SCR conducting; latching current (larger) is needed to turn it on.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Total power in a balanced 3-phase load is:
questions.solution: P = √3·VL·IL·cos φ in terms of line quantities.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The synchronous speed of a 6-pole machine on a 50 Hz supply is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: When a capacitor is added to correct the power factor of an inductive load, which statement is INCORRECT?
options: The reactive power drawn from the supply decreases.
The real power (kW) drawn by the load increases.
questions.solution: Power-factor correction leaves the load's real power unchanged; it only reduces reactive power, current and kVA.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the series RLC circuit shown (L = 0.1 H, C = 40 µF), the resonant frequency is about:
questions.solution: At resonance XL = XC, so f₀ = 1/(2π√(LC)) = 1/(2π√(0.1×40µF)) ≈ 79.6 Hz.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" width="300" height="90"><line x1="10" y1="45" x2="40" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="40" y="37" width="34" height="16" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="57" y="32" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">R</text><path d="M74 45 q8 -12 16 0 q8 -12 16 0 q8 -12 16 0" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="98" y="30" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">L=0.1H</text><line x1="122" y1="45" x2="150" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="150" y1="35" x2="150" y2="55" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="158" y1="35" x2="158" y2="55" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><text x="154" y="30" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">C=40µF</text><line x1="158" y1="45" x2="290" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="290" y1="45" x2="290" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="45" x2="10" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="75" x2="290" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="150" cy="75" r="3" fill="#1e293b"/><text x="150" y="88" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#475569">~ AC source</text></svg>
figure.caption: Series RLC circuit

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 3-phase induction motor at standstill has a slip of:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Regarding a synchronous motor, which statement is INCORRECT?
options: Over-excitation makes it draw leading current.
It runs at exactly synchronous speed in steady state.
It can be used to improve system power factor.
It is inherently self-starting from rest.
questions.solution: A synchronous motor produces no net starting torque at standstill, so it is not self-starting.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a load with R = 3 Ω and reactance X = 6 Ω, the power factor is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The skin effect in an AC conductor causes:
options: Current to crowd toward the surface, raising effective resistance
Current to crowd at the centre
questions.solution: At higher frequency current concentrates near the surface, increasing the effective AC resistance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a divider, 48 V is applied across 4 Ω and 6 Ω in series. The voltage across the 6 Ω resistor is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At series resonance, the impedance of an RLC circuit is:
questions.solution: XL and XC cancel, leaving only R, so impedance is minimum and resistive (current maximum).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the two-wattmeter method on a balanced 3-phase load, the two readings are equal when the power factor is:
questions.solution: At unity pf the phase angle is zero and both wattmeters read equal positive power; at pf 0.5 one reads zero.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the divider shown (12 V applied), the output voltage Vout across R2 is:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 220 150" width="220" height="150"><line x1="30" y1="20" x2="30" y2="50" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="22" y="50" width="16" height="34" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="50" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">R1=3Ω</text><line x1="30" y1="84" x2="30" y2="100" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="22" y="100" width="16" height="34" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="50" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R2=8Ω</text><line x1="30" y1="134" x2="30" y2="145" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="30" cy="92" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="40" y="96" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Vout</text><text x="35" y="16" font-size="10" fill="#475569">12V</text></svg>
figure.caption: Resistive voltage divider

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two identical resistors are connected first in series and then in parallel across the same battery. Which is correct?
options: The series combination dissipates more power.
The parallel combination dissipates four times the power of the series combination.
questions.solution: Series R_eq = 2R (P = V²/2R); parallel R_eq = R/2 (P = 2V²/R) — four times greater for the same voltage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The balanced Wheatstone bridge shown has P = 100 Ω, Q = 10 Ω and S = 30 Ω (galvanometer reads zero). The unknown resistance R is:
questions.solution: At balance P/Q = R/S, so R = P·S/Q = 100×30/10 = 300 Ω.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 200 160" width="200" height="160"><polygon points="100,20 180,80 100,140 20,80" fill="none" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="55" y="45" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">P=100Ω</text><text x="140" y="45" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">Q=10Ω</text><text x="55" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R=?</text><text x="135" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">S=30Ω</text><circle cx="100" cy="80" r="9" fill="none" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="100" y="84" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">G</text></svg>
figure.caption: Wheatstone bridge

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The resonant frequency of a series RLC circuit is given by:
questions.solution: At resonance XL = XC, giving f₀ = 1/(2π√(LC)).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For an ideal buck (step-down) DC–DC converter in continuous conduction, the output voltage is:
questions.solution: A buck converter gives Vo = D·Vs; the boost converter gives Vs/(1−D).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A balanced 3-phase load draws 20 A at 440 V line, pf 0.9. The total power is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Eddy-current loss in a magnetic core varies with frequency f and flux density Bm as:
questions.solution: Eddy loss ∝ f²Bm²t² (lamination thickness t); hysteresis loss ∝ f·Bm^1.6 (Steinmetz).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The inductive reactance of a 0.02 H inductor at 50 Hz is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a purely inductive AC circuit, the current:
options: Is in phase with the voltage
questions.solution: An ideal inductor makes current lag voltage by 90°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A transformer operates on the principle of:
questions.solution: Changing flux from the primary links the secondary, inducing an EMF (mutual induction).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a divider, 48 V is applied across 2 Ω and 4 Ω in series. The voltage across the 4 Ω resistor is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy stored in a capacitor of capacitance C charged to voltage V is:
questions.solution: Energy = ½CV², stored in the electric field between the plates.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The balanced Wheatstone bridge shown has P = 100 Ω, Q = 10 Ω and S = 60 Ω (galvanometer reads zero). The unknown resistance R is:
questions.solution: At balance P/Q = R/S, so R = P·S/Q = 100×60/10 = 600 Ω.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 200 160" width="200" height="160"><polygon points="100,20 180,80 100,140 20,80" fill="none" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="55" y="45" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">P=100Ω</text><text x="140" y="45" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">Q=10Ω</text><text x="55" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R=?</text><text x="135" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">S=60Ω</text><circle cx="100" cy="80" r="9" fill="none" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="100" y="84" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">G</text></svg>

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The form factor of a sinusoidal waveform is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 100 W bulb and a 60 W bulb (rated 230 V) are connected in series across 230 V. Which glows brighter?
questions.solution: The 60 W bulb has higher resistance, so in series it drops more voltage and dissipates more power — it glows brighter.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A series RLC circuit has R = 10 Ω, L = 0.2 H, C = 40 µF. Its quality factor Q is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A DC series motor should never be started:
questions.solution: On no load a series motor races to dangerously high speed because flux falls with the small current.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the series RLC circuit shown (L = 0.5 H, C = 10 µF), the resonant frequency is about:
questions.solution: At resonance XL = XC, so f₀ = 1/(2π√(LC)) = 1/(2π√(0.5×10µF)) ≈ 71.2 Hz.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ohm's law states that, at constant temperature, the current through a conductor is:
options: Proportional to the square of the voltage
Directly proportional to the voltage across it
questions.solution: V = IR, so for constant R the current rises in direct proportion to the applied voltage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kirchhoff's Voltage Law (KVL) is a statement of the conservation of:
questions.solution: The sum of voltage drops around a closed loop equals zero — energy supplied equals energy absorbed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two resistors of 4 Ω and 6 Ω are connected in parallel. The equivalent resistance is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Power factor is defined as the cosine of the angle between:
questions.solution: Power factor = cos φ, where φ is the phase angle between voltage and current.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The synchronous speed of a 4-pole machine on a 60 Hz supply is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Transformers are rated in kVA rather than kW because:
options: Their losses depend on voltage and current, not on the load power factor
questions.solution: Iron loss depends on voltage and copper loss on current; both are independent of the load power factor, so apparent power (kVA) is the rating.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Electrical power dissipated in a resistor can be written as:
questions.solution: P = VI = I²R = V²/R for a resistor.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The impedance triangle shown has R = 4 Ω and X = 4 Ω. The impedance phase angle is about:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 200 140" width="200" height="140"><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="180" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="20" y2="10" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="140" y2="120" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="90" y="134" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R=4Ω</text><line x1="140" y1="120" x2="140" y2="50" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="145" y="85" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">X=4Ω</text><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="140" y2="50" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="70" y="78" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">Z</text></svg>

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two resistors of 4 Ω and 6 Ω in series give a total resistance of:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A transformer with 100 primary and 400 secondary turns is fed 1100 V. The secondary voltage is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Thevenin's theorem replaces a linear network by:
options: A current source in parallel with a resistance
A voltage source in series with a resistance
questions.solution: Thevenin: an equivalent EMF Vth in series with Rth seen from the terminals.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The back EMF of a DC motor is:
questions.solution: Eb = (PφZN)/(60A); it rises with speed and flux and opposes the supply.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The current-versus-frequency curve of a series RLC circuit peaks at the marked frequency f₀ ≈ 60 Hz. At this resonant frequency the power factor is:
questions.solution: At series resonance XL = XC, the impedance is purely resistive and current peaks, so the power factor is unity. (Here f₀ ≈ 60 Hz.)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The superposition theorem is applicable only to:
questions.solution: Superposition holds for linear, bilateral networks; responses of independent sources add.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A current of 4 A flows through a 10 Ω resistor. The power dissipated is:
